# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

Resources:
   EC2Instance1: # First instance, replace 'xyz', change name according to requirement if needed or use dynamic references e.g., !Ref AWS::StackName-CustomResourceExtensionUniqueId  etc... can be used for unique ids per each resource type (e.g.: MyFirstEC2Instance)
     Type: "AWS::EC2::Instance" # instance is the required service in EC2, this will create an new ec2 server with user-data enabled on it so that we have shell script to perform our tasks during deployment time and make sure all resources are accessible over internet.  This requires AWS_KeyName parameter defined
     Properties:  
       ImageId: ami-0abef6f987e4bc523 # latest Ubuntu Server image required for EC2 instances with User Data to execute prerequisites tasks on instance creation e.g., software updates, upgrades etc...  replace by actual AMI id if needed or use dynamic references
       InstanceType: t2.micro   # The micro option will be good enough but more can accommodate higher performance when necessary in the future (t3), depending upon need and cost of EC2 instances used as per AWS pricing structure for Micro, Tiny etc...  replace by actual instance type if needed or use dynamic references
       MinCount: '1'    # minimum number required e.g., to keep up running smoothly even when a single resource fails we require at least one more ec2 server here in case the other servers fail over time and do not run correctly (Include auto-scaling group).  replace by actual count if needed or use dynamic references
       MaxCount: '1'    # maximum instances, depends on your need to scale out. If you want only single instance but also provide backup options then set it as "0", depending upon requirement make sure in future scaling of resources accordingly e.. (Include auto-scaling group)  replace by actual count if needed or use dynamic references
       NetworkInterfaces: # define the network interfaces for EC2 instances and mount an EBS volume. Make all subnets public to allow internet access so that our application server can be accessed from anywhere through browser e., !Ref PrivateSubnet1,!ImportValue('/subnet/private') etc...  replace by actual references if needed
       VolumeSize: '8' # size of the EBS volumes in GB required (Replace with your specific requirement)   Replace this value based on instance type and region needs. It depends upon EC2 instances need for storage as per AWS pricing structure, e.. Include all regions available under each AZs
       Volumes:  # define volume details i.e., the Amazon EBS snapshot ID where you want to attach an IAM role with which our instance can access this EBS Volume   Replace by actual values if needed or use dynamic references like !Ref SnapshotID,!ImportValue('/snapshots/mySnapshot') etc...
         - DeviceName: '/dev/sdh'  # required device name for ebs volume i.e., /dev/sdf is the common convention used in Amazon EBS volumes and you should refer to your instance’s AWS_BlockDeviceMapping parameter (Reference AWSCLI) or user-data field
           VolumesPerInstance: '1'  # required, this indicates we have only one volume per EC2 Instance. If there is a need for additional storage then provide more instances like !Ref VolumeSize ,!Add here... etc.... Replace by actual values if needed to suit requirement otherwise leave it as dynamic
         - SnapshotId: 'snap-0abcdef123456789a' # required, e.g., snapshot id of the EBS volume from your existing snapshots list  replace with reference here... or use AWSCLI command for instance creation to dynamically generate this
           Encrypted: True   # enable/disable encryption on our volumes by default it is true so that data can be safeguarded at rest and in transit. You may choose not encrypt if the volume's access needs are publicly exposed e.. Replace with actual boolean value  replace here... etc.... dynamically set
           DeleteOnTermination: True # required, enable/disable deletion of EBS Volumes when instance termination is triggered (replace by bool ref) or user-data can handle this if needed. If it's not defined in cloudformation then use default setting which will be true automatically at end  replace here... etc.... dynamically set
           VirtualName: 'myvolume1' # name of the EBS volume i.e., define a suitable unique logical identifier for each block-device mapping e.. Replace by actual value or dynamic references if needed else use default values in cloudformation template as per AWS documentation  replace here... etc.... dynamically set
       IamInstanceProfile: !GetAtt EC2InstanceRole . Arn # required, reference the created role and provide an instance profile to allow our instances to assume this role e.g., Replace with ref if needed or use default value in cloudformation template else it will be auto-created by AWS as a new Role each time
       InstanceProfile: !GetAtt EC2InstanceRole . Arn # required, reference the created IAM instance profile and provide an ec2 instances assume role policy e.e., Replace with ref if needed or use default value in cloudformation template else it will be auto-created by AWS as a new Role each time
       KeyName: !Ref 'AWS::EC2::KeyPair' # required, reference the EC2 key pair (replace name of actual keypair here) and assign to ec instances e.e., Replace with ref if needed or use default value in cloudformation template else it will be auto-created by AWS as a new Role each time
       Subnets: !ImportValue '/subnet/private' # required, reference the private subnet for EC2 Instances and make all available instances within this public IPs e.e., Replace with ref if needed or use default value in cloudformation template else it will be auto-created by AWS as a new Role each time
         - AvailabilityZone: !Select '0' # required, specify availability zones for your EC2 instance (replace available Zones)  replace here... etc.... dynamically set based on requirement or AZs list from cloudformation template if you know it. If not known then let AWS choose the best one
       Tags:    Add any relevant tags that are needed e., !Ref 'AWS::StackName'   # name of your CloudFormation stack, replace by actual value  Replace here... etc.... dynamically set based on requirement or cloudformation template if you know it. If not known then let AWS choose the best one
       VolumeType: ebs-gp2    # required type for EBS volumes (replace with supported types), as per list of allowed Amazon EC2 volume sizes and types, https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instance-store-ephemeral.html
         - DeviceName: '/dev/sdh' # required device name for ebs volumes i., replace by actual values if needed or use dynamic references  dynamically set based on requirement and cloudformation template settings as per AWS documentation, Replace here... etc.... all in the above steps with corresponding value replacements.
